perturbation.original_text: Our Agreement with the Seller requires that we obtain a non-disclosure and confidentiality agreement and evidence of financial ability before disclosing the name and location of his business. This information will be kept confidential.
perturbation.changed_text: Our Agreement with the Vendor requires that we obtain a non-disclosure and confidentiality agreement and evidence of financial ability before disclosing the name and location of his business. This information will be kept private.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Seller' is replaced with 'Vendor' and 'confidential' is replaced with 'private'. While seemingly synonymous, this introduces ambiguity. The agreement initially states information will be 'kept confidential' implying a legal obligation, but then uses 'private', which can imply a weaker, more subjective standard. It is unclear if 'Vendor' and 'Seller' are the same entity. This contradiction affects how strictly the confidentiality clause is interpreted and enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Our Agreement with the Vendor requires that we obtain a non-disclosure and confidentiality agreement and evidence of financial ability before disclosing the name and location of his business. This information will be kept private. In compliance with the above, please read and comp lete the following Non-Disclosure and Confidentiality Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Seller' to 'Vendor' and 'confidential' to 'private' in the changed text introduces ambiguity regarding the parties involved and the level of protection afforded to the disclosed information. The original text uses 'Seller' and 'confidential', implying a specific legal obligation. The changed text, using 'Vendor' and 'private', could be interpreted as a weaker standard of protection. Because the contradicted text also contains the changed terms, the original intention and obligation are unclear creating a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: That I will not disclose any Information re garding these businesses to any other person who has not also signed and dated this agreement, except to secure their advice and counsel, in which case I agree to obtain their consent to maintain such confidentiality. “Information” shall include the f act that the business is for sale plus other data.
perturbation.changed_text: That I will not disclose any Data re garding these businesses to any other person who has not also signed and dated this agreement, except to secure their advice and counsel, in which case I agree to obtain their consent to maintain such privacy. “Information” shall include the f act that the business is for sale plus other data.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Information' is replaced with 'Data' and 'confidentiality' with 'privacy', while the definition of Information still includes 'the fact that the business is for sale plus other data'. It's unclear if 'Data' encompasses the same scope as 'Information' as defined later, introducing uncertainty about what is protected. This creates a contradiction: earlier the user agrees to not disclose data, but 'data' is not accurately being represented due to the 'Information' definition later on. This ambiguity can lead to disputes over what the potential investor is obligated to keep private.
perturbation.contradicted_text: That I will not disclose any Data re garding these businesses to any other person who has not also signed and dated this agreement, except to secure their advice and counsel, in which case I agree to obtain their consent to maintain such privacy. “Information” shall include the f act that the business is for sale plus other data. The term Information does not include any information, which is, or becomes, generally available to the public or is already in your possession.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Information' with 'Data' and 'confidentiality' with 'privacy'. The contradicted text uses 'Data' but retains the definition of 'Information', which creates a misalignment because the definition associated with data is still referring to information. Since the altered text replaces 'Information' with 'Data' while keeping the original definition of 'Information', this means that 'Data' is defined in terms of 'Information', creating a contradiction that fits the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition because the document is using 'Information' and 'Data' interchangeably without a clear and consistent understanding.

perturbation.original_text: That all Information is provided by the Seller and is not verified in any way by Tameracq Partners, Inc. Tameracq Partners, Inc is relying on Seller for the accuracy and completeness of said Information, has no knowle dge of the accuracy of said Information and makes no warranty, expressed or implied, as to such Information.
perturbation.changed_text: That all Data is provided by the Proprietor and is not verified in any way by Tameracq Partners, Inc. Tameracq Partners, Inc is relying on Proprietor for the accuracy and completeness of said Data, has no knowle dge of the accuracy of said Data and makes no warranty, expressed or implied, as to such Data.
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'Information' and 'Seller' are replaced with 'Data' and 'Proprietor' respectively. This change suggests a different source and scope of information, contradicting the defined term 'Information' in previous sections, and the previously mentioned 'Seller'. It is unclear if 'Data' is the same 'Information', and if 'Proprietor' is the same 'Seller', leading to confusion about the extent of Tameracq's disclaimer of responsibility.
perturbation.contradicted_text: That I will not disclose any Data re garding these businesses to any other person who has not also signed and dated this agreement, except to secure their advice and counsel, in which case I agree to obtain their consent to maintain such privacy. “Information” shall include the f act that the business is for sale plus other data. The term Information does not include any information, which is, or becomes, generally available to the public or is already in your possession.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Information' provided by the 'Seller'. The changed text substitutes these terms with 'Data' and 'Proprietor'. The contradicted text defines 'Information', and it is now unclear whether this definition applies to 'Data'. If 'Data' is distinct from 'Information', the obligations regarding non-disclosure may not apply. The perturbation explanation states that 'It is unclear if 'Data' is the same 'Information', and if 'Proprietor' is the same 'Seller', leading to confusion about the extent of Tameracq's disclaimer of responsibility.' Since the document uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, and this leads to conflicting obligations (definition of Data vs Information), it aligns with 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: I agree that Tameracq Partners, Inc is not responsible for the accuracy of any Informati on I received and I agree to indemnify and hold Tameracq Partners, Inc harmless from any claims or damages resulting from its use. I will look only to Seller and to my own investigation for all information regarding any business offered by the Franchisee.
perturbation.changed_text: I agree that Tameracq Partners, Inc is not responsible for the accuracy of any Data I received and I agree to indemnify and hold Tameracq Partners, Inc harmless from any claims or damages resulting from its use. I will look only to Vendor and to my own investigation for all data regarding any business offered by the Franchisee.
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'Information' and 'Seller' are replaced with 'Data' and 'Vendor' respectively. These changes create uncertainty about the scope of the indemnity and where the potential investor should seek reliable information. The substitution with 'Data' and 'Vendor' may suggest a limitation of liability to a specific subset of information or a specific entity, which differs from the overall understanding. This creates an in-text contradiction: earlier 'Information' had a definition, now it's 'Data'. Same with the Seller and Vendor.
perturbation.contradicted_text: That I will not disclose any Data re garding these businesses to any other person who has not also signed and dated this agreement, except to secure their advice and counsel, in which case I agree to obtain their consent to maintain such privacy. “Information” shall include the f act that the business is for sale plus other data. The term Information does not include any information, which is, or becomes, generally available to the public or is already in your possession.
perturbation.justification: The original text used 'Information' and 'Seller'. The changed text replaces these with 'Data' and 'Vendor'. The contradicted text defines 'Information' to include the fact that the business is for sale plus other data. The change creates a contradiction because the definition of 'Information' in the contradicted text now conflicts with the use of 'Data' in the changed text, creating uncertainty about whether the disclosure restriction applies to the same scope of information.

perturbation.original_text: That, should I enter into an agreement to pur chase a business the Franchisee offers for sale, I grant to the Seller the right to obtain, through standard reporting agencies, financial and credit information concerning my self or the companies or other parties I represent and understand that this informa tion will be held conf idential by Seller and Tameracq Partners, Inc and will only be used for the purpose of Seller extending credit to me.
perturbation.changed_text: That, should I enter into an agreement to pur chase a business the Franchisee offers for sale, I grant to the Vendor the right to obtain, through standard reporting agencies, financial and credit data concerning my self or the companies or other parties I represent and understand that this privacy will be held secure by Vendor and Tameracq Partners, Inc and will only be used for the purpose of Vendor extending credit to me.
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'Seller', 'information' and 'confidential' are replaced with 'Vendor', 'data' and 'secure' respectively. The shift from 'confidential' to 'secure' weakens the level of protection promised. Using 'data' instead of 'information' also creates ambiguity. Also, there is confusion on what is the definition of vendor at this point, since it started as 'Seller'. These create contradictions about what is kept safe and by whom, because it is not consistent to the previous terms and creates ambiguity of definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: That I will not disclose any Data re garding these businesses to any other person who has not also signed and dated this agreement, except to secure their advice and counsel, in which case I agree to obtain their consent to maintain such privacy. “Information” shall include the f act that the business is for sale plus other data. The term Information does not include any information, which is, or becomes, generally available to the public or is already in your possession.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Seller' with 'Vendor', 'information' with 'data' and 'confidential' with 'secure'. The contradicted text defines 'Information' and discusses maintaining 'privacy'. The change from 'confidential' to 'secure' weakens the protection promised, and the use of 'data' creates ambiguity, contradicting the explicit definition of 'Information' in the contradicted text. Furthermore, the replacement of seller with vendor introduces the type of contradiction described by the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, which is when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations, because the 'Seller' and 'Vendor' were never defined, therefore, leading to the contradiction.
